steps: Press the "lock" button once (to be confirmed).
Press the "lock" button on the digital key or remote control key fob.
notes: If the vehicle was locked with the remote control, it cannot be unlocked touchless.
If the vehicle has been idle for several days, touchless unlocking and locking is not possible until after the vehicle has been driven again.

steps: Lock the car by pressing the button on the remote control key (to be confirmed).

methods.text: The "sleeping key fob" feature automatically deactivates the transmitter when the key fob has not moved for 40 seconds.
Disable "Passive entry" in the car's menu.

steps: Lock the car.
On the key fob, press and hold the lock and unlock buttons together for about 4 seconds.
The hazard lights flash 4 times to confirm the fob is disabled.
notes: While disabled, touching the door handle will not unlock the car. Press any fob button to re-enable.
Wording and availability vary by model and year -- check your owner's manual.

methods.models: Models with this menu (e.g. Ioniq, Kona, Tucson)
steps: On the infotainment screen, go to Settings > Vehicle > Door.
Turn "Approach unlock" (walk-up unlock) off.
notes: This stops the car unlocking automatically as you approach, but the door-handle button may still work.

steps: Open the driver's door (using the fob).
Place the key fob inside the car.
Press the headlamp button on the key fob 3 times.
Press the unlock button on the key fob once.
Take the keys outside the car.
Close the driver's door.
Lock the car using the fob.
Unlock the car using the fob.
notes: To re-enable it, follow the same procedure but press the lock button at step 4.
You must first unlock and then re-lock the car with the fob -- not the keyless button on the door handle.

steps: Lock the car using the button on the door handle.
On the key fob, press and hold the lock and unlock buttons together.
The car's indicators flash once, pause, then flash 4 times -- the fob is now disabled.
notes: While disabled, the car will not open from the door-handle button. Press any fob button to re-enable.
Availability varies by model and year.

methods.models: Models with this menu (e.g. EV6, Sportage, Niro)
steps: On the infotainment screen, go to Settings > Vehicle > Door.
Turn "Approach unlock" off.
Press and hold the "lock" button.
While still holding it, press the "unlock" button twice.
The light on the fob flashes 4 times to confirm keyless entry is now disabled.
notes: To turn keyless entry back on, press any button on the fob.
This is the same battery-saving sleep used by Toyota; it also blocks relay attacks while active.

methods.text: Turning on the "transmitter power saving function" switches off the advanced keyless entry and push-button start, and limits the fob's battery use.

sub.label: To turn the power saving function ON
steps: Press the lock button on the fob 4 times within 3 seconds -- the indicator light turns on.
While the indicator light is on (about 5 seconds), press and hold the lock button for 1.5 seconds or longer.
Press any button on the fob to check the indicator light does not turn on or flash -- this confirms it's off.

sub.label: To turn the power saving function OFF
steps: Press the lock button on the fob 4 times within 3 seconds -- the indicator light turns on.
While the indicator light is on (about 5 seconds), press and hold the lock button for 1.5 seconds or longer.

notes: Once this is on, the car won't start with the key until you either turn power-save mode off, or hold the key against the start/stop button while pressing it.

steps: Lock the car as usual.
Press the lock button again twice, quickly.
Go to Settings > Vehicle > Locking.
Change the i-key setting to "off".

methods.text: The latest key fobs turn themselves off after a period of inactivity.
Nissan calls this feature "i-key" (intelligent key).

steps: Press and hold the lock button.
At the same time, press the unlock button within 1 second.
Keep both buttons pressed until the indicator light on the key lights up continuously.
With the key inside the car and the door open,
press the headlight button 3 times, then the unlock button.
Then lock the car with the fob as normal.
Press the "lock" button once (to be confirmed).
On the centre touchscreen, go to the Settings tab.
Open the Doors & Locks menu.
Switch 'Passive Entry' OFF (you must press the brake pedal).

methods.models: Most models with Smart Key (push-button start)
steps: Press and hold the lock button on the key fob.
While still holding lock, press the unlock button twice.
The light on the fob flashes 4 times to confirm keyless entry is now disabled.
notes: To turn keyless entry back on, press any button on the fob.
This puts the fob into a battery-saving sleep that also blocks relay attacks: touching the door handle or pressing start will not work until you wake the fob.
A Toyota dealer can disable the Smart Key system permanently if you prefer.

steps: Lock the car with the key fob.
Then press the little indented area on the door handle.
Keyless entry is now disabled until you next unlock the car.

methods.text: The key fob goes to sleep after 15 minutes of inactivity.
Volkswagen calls its keyless entry feature "KESSY".

steps: On the centre touchscreen, go to Settings > Vehicle.
Open Remote Lock/Unlock/Start (or Power Door Locks).
Turn off Passive Unlocking (and Passive Locking).
notes: This stops the car unlocking when you touch the handle with the fob nearby.

steps: On the key fob, press and hold the lock and unlock buttons together for about 4 seconds.
This toggles keyless (passive) entry off. Repeat to turn it back on.
On the centre touchscreen, go to Settings > Vehicle.
Open Remote Lock/Unlock/Start (or Power Door Locks).
Turn off Passive Unlocking (and Passive Locking).
notes: This stops the car unlocking when you touch the handle with the fob nearby.

steps: On the key fob, press and hold the lock and unlock buttons together for about 4 seconds.
This toggles keyless (passive) entry off. Repeat to turn it back on.
On the centre touchscreen, go to Settings > Vehicle.
Open Remote Lock/Unlock/Start (or Power Door Locks).
Turn off Passive Unlocking (and Passive Locking).
notes: This stops the car unlocking when you touch the handle with the fob nearby.

steps: On the key fob, press and hold the lock and unlock buttons together for about 4 seconds.
This toggles keyless (passive) entry off. Repeat to turn it back on.
On the centre touchscreen, go to Settings > Vehicle.
Open Remote Lock/Unlock/Start (or Power Door Locks).
Turn off Passive Unlocking (and Passive Locking).
notes: This stops the car unlocking when you touch the handle with the fob nearby.

steps: On the key fob, press and hold the lock and unlock buttons together for about 4 seconds.
This toggles keyless (passive) entry off. Repeat to turn it back on.
On the Uconnect touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (or Personal Settings).
Turn off Passive Entry (Keyless Enter-N-Go).
notes: You can still lock/unlock with the fob buttons; only the touch-the-handle feature is disabled.

steps: On the Uconnect touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (or Personal Settings).
Turn off Passive Entry (Keyless Enter-N-Go).
notes: You can still lock/unlock with the fob buttons; only the touch-the-handle feature is disabled.

steps: On the Uconnect touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (or Personal Settings).
Turn off Passive Entry (Keyless Enter-N-Go).
notes: You can still lock/unlock with the fob buttons; only the touch-the-handle feature is disabled.

steps: On the Uconnect touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (or Personal Settings).
Turn off Passive Entry (Keyless Enter-N-Go).
notes: You can still lock/unlock with the fob buttons; only the touch-the-handle feature is disabled.

methods.text: Like Ford, recent Lincoln intelligent-access fobs go to sleep after a period without movement, which blocks relay attacks overnight.

steps: In the SYNC settings, open the door/lock settings.
Turn off "Intelligent Access" / passive entry.
In the centre screen, open Settings.
Under the locks/keys section, turn off passive (proximity) entry so the car only unlocks from the app or key fob button.
notes: If you use the phone as a key, also consider turning off passive unlock for the phone.

steps: In the centre display, open Settings.
Under doors/locks, turn off passive (walk-up) unlocking.

methods.text: Honda's Smart Entry proximity unlock has no simple fob on/off button. You can switch off "Walk Away Auto Lock" in the vehicle settings menu, but fully disabling proximity unlocking usually needs a Honda dealer or an OBD tool (e.g. Carista).
notes: Until disabled, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight to block relay attacks.
Locking/unlocking with the fob buttons and push-button start are unaffected.

methods.text: Honda calls this "Smart Entry with Walk Away Auto Lock".
Acura uses Honda's Smart Entry system. There is no simple fob on/off; "Walk Away Auto Lock" can be turned off in settings, but fully disabling proximity unlock generally needs a dealer or OBD tool.
notes: Until disabled, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight to block relay attacks.

steps: In the vehicle settings menu, open the lock/Intelligent Key settings.
Turn the Intelligent Key (i-Key) proximity feature off.
notes: Infiniti uses Nissan's Intelligent Key system, so the menu is similar to Nissan's.

steps: Lock the car.
On the key fob, press and hold the lock and unlock buttons together for about 4 seconds.
The hazard lights flash to confirm the fob is disabled.
notes: Genesis uses Hyundai's smart-key system. Press any fob button to re-enable.
Availability varies by model and year.

steps: On the infotainment screen, go to Settings > Vehicle > Door.
Turn "Approach unlock" off.
In the vehicle settings, open the keyless/locking options.
Turn off the keyless operation (proximity) function where available.
notes: Mitsubishi calls this the "Keyless Operation System" (KOS / FAST key). On models without a menu option, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight.

methods.text: Suzuki's keyless push-start system has no widely documented on/off switch. Until one is confirmed for your model, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight to block relay attacks, and check your owner's manual under "keyless start".

Volvo's keyless entry cannot be fully switched off from the car's menus -- the settings only let you choose which doors unlock. To block relay attacks, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch, or ask a Volvo dealer about disabling Keyless Drive.

steps: In the centre display, open Settings.
Under locking, turn off keyless/passive unlock if your model offers it.
notes: Polestar shares Volvo's platform; on models where it can't be turned off, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch.
Polestar's phone-as-key feature should also be reviewed if you use it.

steps: On the touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (or Personal Settings).
Turn off Passive Entry / hands-free keyless entry.
notes: You can still use the fob buttons; only touch-the-handle entry is disabled. On models without this menu option, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight.

steps: On the touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (or Personal Settings).
Turn off Passive Entry / hands-free keyless entry.
notes: You can still use the fob buttons; only touch-the-handle entry is disabled. On models without this menu option, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight.

steps: On the touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (or Personal Settings).
Turn off Passive Entry / hands-free keyless entry.
notes: You can still use the fob buttons; only touch-the-handle entry is disabled. On models without this menu option, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight.

steps: On the touchscreen (MTC), open Settings.
Under doors/locks, turn off passive keyless entry where available.
notes: To block relay attacks, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight and check your owner's manual under "keyless" entry/start.

steps: On the touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (or Personal Settings).
Turn off Passive Entry / hands-free keyless entry.
notes: You can still use the fob buttons; only touch-the-handle entry is disabled. On models without this menu option, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight.

steps: On the touchscreen, open Settings.
Go to Doors & Locks (or Personal Settings).
Turn off Passive Entry / hands-free keyless entry.
notes: You can still use the fob buttons; only touch-the-handle entry is disabled. On models without this menu option, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight.

steps: Lock the car with the key fob.
Then press the small indented area on the door handle.
Keyless entry is now disabled until you next unlock the car with the fob.
notes: This mirrors Volkswagen's KESSY system used across the VW Group.

methods.text: Newer VW Group fobs go to sleep after about 15 minutes without movement, which blocks relay attacks overnight.

steps: Lock the car with the key fob.
Then press the small indented area on the door handle.
Keyless entry is now disabled until you next unlock the car with the fob.
notes: This mirrors Volkswagen's KESSY system used across the VW Group.

methods.text: Newer VW Group fobs go to sleep after about 15 minutes without movement, which blocks relay attacks overnight.

steps: In the vehicle settings menu, open the access/locking options.
Turn off the hands-free keyless card ("mains libres") proximity unlocking where available.
notes: Renault uses a hands-free key card. On models without a menu option, keep the card in a Faraday pouch overnight.

methods.text: Most Dacia models use a conventional remote (press to unlock), not proximity keyless, so they are generally not vulnerable to relay attacks. If your Dacia has hands-free keyless entry, To block relay attacks, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight and check your owner's manual under "keyless" entry/start.

steps: Lock the car as usual.
Press the lock button again twice, quickly.
notes: Older Smart cars share Mercedes' keyless system. Newer Smart #1/#3 are built with Geely -- check the car's menu instead.

methods.text: For keyless Lotus models (e.g. Emira, Eletre), no simple on/off is widely documented. To block relay attacks, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight and check your owner's manual under "keyless" entry/start.
No simple keyless on/off is widely documented for Aston Martin. To block relay attacks, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight and check your owner's manual under "keyless" entry/start.
No simple keyless on/off is widely documented for McLaren. To block relay attacks, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight and check your owner's manual under "keyless" entry/start.
Rolls-Royce uses BMW key technology; recent fobs have a motion sensor that puts the key to sleep when left still, blocking relay attacks overnight.
notes: Locking the car with the fob also disables touchless unlocking, as on BMW.

methods.text: Lamborghini uses VW Group (Audi) electronics. The Urus may offer KESSY-style behaviour similar to Audi; otherwise To block relay attacks, keep the fob in a Faraday pouch overnight and check your owner's manual under "keyless" entry/start.
